title: On - - - - Asleep.
content:
Sleep on, and dream of Heav'n awhile.
    Tho' shut so close thy laughing eyes,
    Thy rosy lips still seem to smile,
    And move, and breathe delicious sighs!--

    Ah, now soft blushes tinge her cheeks,
    And mantle o'er her neck of snow.
    Ah, now she murmurs, now she speaks
    What most I wish--and fear to know.

    She starts, she trembles, and she weeps!
    Her fair hands folded on her breast.
    --And now, how like a saint she sleeps!
    A seraph in the realms of rest!

    Sleep on secure! Above controul,
    Thy thoughts belong to Heav'n and thee!
    And may the secret of thy soul
    Repose within its sanctuary!
meaning:
Exploring the themes of classic, Samuel Rogers delivers a powerful performance in "On - - - - Asleep."...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
